title: Comparative UX Audit
tagline: Compare the same flow across 3 competing products using Nielsen's heuristics.
scenario: A product team is redesigning their sign-up flow. Before starting, they want to understand how competitors handle the same task. Your job is to audit 3 competitors and extract the best patterns.
brief: Pick a common user flow (sign-up, checkout, or onboarding). Audit 3 competing products that all have this flow. Score each against Nielsen's 10 heuristics and recommend which patterns to adopt.
deliverables: A comparison table: Product × Heuristic score (1-5) for at least 6 of Nielsen's 10 heuristics
Three specific UX patterns worth stealing, with annotated descriptions of how each competitor implements them
Two anti-patterns to avoid, with explanation of why they fail
submissionGuidance: Do not just list heuristic violations. The value is in the comparative lens — what does Product A do better than B, and why? Be specific about screens and interactions.

rubric.label: Heuristic Application
rubric.description: Are the heuristics correctly applied and scored consistently across products?

rubric.label: Pattern Extraction
rubric.description: Are the recommended patterns specific and well-described?

rubric.label: Anti-Pattern Analysis
rubric.description: Are the anti-patterns genuine failures (not just preferences)?
